data.description:
## Why
On failure the bot must NEVER land a partial/inconsistent stack and NEVER evict a change from within a stack (that would orphan its descendants). The whole stack returns to its owning agent to remediate — this is the one Goal-2 exception (agent pulled in only when code must change).
## What
Handle the two failure modes in the auto-lander loop: CI-fail (post-rebase `Verified -1`) and rebase-conflict (`POST /rebase` `409`). The recorded outcome is the single source of truth consumed by the agent contract.
## Scope
Failure/edge paths of the core-loop service; the recorded outcome feeds the agent contract story.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CI-fail: on a post-rebase `Verified -1`, remove `Autosubmit`, stop driving the stack; post NO bot comment (the native `-1` + run logs are the signal — no redundant channel).
- [ ] Rebase-conflict: on `POST /rebase` `409`, remove `Autosubmit` and record a `needs_rebase` outcome event-driven on that change only (NOT a continuously-maintained up-to-date vote — that would churn O(open) per landing).
- [ ] Never partial-land; never evict a change from within a stack — a failure anywhere in a stack returns the WHOLE stack to its owning agent.
- [ ] Never `allow_conflicts` (no conflict-marker patchsets ever submitted).
- [ ] The recorded outcome is the authoritative source consumed by the agent contract; state transitions are idempotent under retries.
data.title: S3 Failure handling & stack-atomic hand-back (409 / CI-fail; never partial-land)
